workflow.name: Send scheduled IELTS grammar, writing and reading practice via Telegram with Gemini AI
workflow.description:
## 🚀 Overview

This workflow automatically generates IELTS practice content using AI and sends it to Telegram on a schedule.

It covers three key IELTS skills:
- 🧠 Grammar Practice (Monday)
- ✍️ Writing Task (Wednesday)
- 📘 Reading Practice (Friday)

The workflow is designed to deliver structured, clean, and readable exercises directly to your Telegram chat.

---

## ⚙️ How it works

1. The Schedule Trigger runs the workflow on specific days
2. The "Select Test by Day" node determines which practice to generate
3. AI generates structured IELTS content in JSON format
4. The output is parsed and formatted into readable messages
5. Long messages are split into chunks
6. The content is sent to Telegram

---

## 🧩 Features

- AI-powered IELTS content generation
- Clean JSON parsing and formatting
- Telegram-ready message delivery
- Automatic scheduling by day
- Supports multiple IELTS skills in one workflow

---

## 🛠 Setup

1. Create a Telegram Bot via @BotFather
2. Copy your Bot Token
3. Add Telegram credentials in n8n
4. Add your AI credentials (Gemini or OpenAI)
5. Replace the Chat ID in the "Send" nodes with your own

---

## 📩 Telegram Setup

To get your Chat ID:
1. Send a message to your bot
2. Open:
   https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/getUpdates
3. Copy the `chat.id`

---

## ⚠️ Important Notes

- This workflow uses a fixed Chat ID for scheduled messages
- Make sure to replace the Chat ID before running
- Do not hardcode sensitive credentials
- Ensure the AI output format remains consistent (JSON)

---

## 💡 Customization

You can easily customize:
- Topics and difficulty level
- Message format
- Schedule timing
- Add new modules (e.g., Speaking, Vocabulary)

---

## 🎯 Use Cases

- IELTS learners who want daily practice
- Teachers sharing exercises with students
- Automated English learning systems
- AI-powered educational bots

---

## 🚀 Creator Note

This workflow demonstrates how to combine AI generation, structured parsing, and messaging automation into a scalable learning system.

Feel free to adapt and extend it for your own use case.

workflow.name: AI-powered IELTS Practice Bot that automatically delivers Grammar, Writing, and Reading exercises via Telegram.

values.content:
=You are an expert IELTS examiner with Band 9 proficiency, specializing in English grammar assessment.

Your task is to generate a complete IELTS-style grammar practice exercise.

TOPIC:
[INSERT TOPIC HERE]

========================
GRAMMAR FOCUS
========================
Focus on common IELTS grammar areas such as:
- Tenses (past, present, perfect)
- Subject-verb agreement
- Articles (a, an, the)
- Prepositions
- Conditionals
- Passive voice
- Relative clauses
- Modals
- Sentence structure

========================
EXERCISE REQUIREMENTS
========================
Create EXACTLY 10 questions.

Include a mix of these types:
- Fill in the blanks
- Error correction
- Sentence transformation
- Multiple choice (grammar-based)
- Sentence rewriting

Each question must:
- Be clear and unambiguous
- Focus on ONE grammar concept
- Be suitable for Band 6.0–7.5 level
- Use natural English (not robotic)

========================
OUTPUT RULES (VERY IMPORTANT)
========================
- Return ONLY valid JSON
- Do NOT include any explanation outside JSON
- Do NOT include markdown formatting

========================
OUTPUT FORMAT
========================

{
  "title": "IELTS Grammar Practice",
  "topic": "[INSERT TOPIC HERE]",
  "grammar_focus": ["Tenses", "Articles", "Prepositions"],
  "questions": [
    {
      "number": 1,
      "type": "Fill in the blank",
      "question": "She _____ (go) to the market yesterday.",
      "options": [],
      "instruction": "Write the correct form of the verb"
    },
    {
      "number": 2,
      "type": "Multiple Choice",
      "question": "He has lived here _____ 2010.",
      "options": ["since", "for", "during", "at"],
      "instruction": "Choose the correct answer"
    }
  ],
  "answer_key": [
    {
      "number": 1,
      "answer": "went"
    },
    {
      "number": 2,
      "answer": "since"
    }
  ],
  "explanations": [
    {
      "number": 1,
      "explanation": "Past simple is used because the action happened yesterday."
    },
    {
      "number": 2,
      "explanation": "'Since' is used with a specific point in time."
    }
  ],
  "next_exercise": "Create 3 similar sentences using past tense and time expressions."
}

=You are an expert IELTS Writing examiner.

Generate one IELTS Writing task.

========================
REQUIREMENTS
========================
- Task type: Task 1 or Task 2
- Use realistic IELTS question style
- Clear instructions
- Suitable for Band 6–7.5

========================
OUTPUT RULES
========================
- Return ONLY valid JSON
- No markdown

========================
OUTPUT FORMAT
========================

{
  "title": "IELTS Writing Practice",
  "task_type": "Task 2",
  "question": "Some people believe that technology makes life easier, while others think it creates more problems. Discuss both views and give your opinion.",
  "instructions": "Write at least 250 words.",
  "tips": [
    "State your opinion clearly",
    "Use examples to support your ideas",
    "Organize your essay into clear paragraphs"
  ]
}

=You are an expert IELTS Academic Reading examiner with Band 9 proficiency.

Your task is to generate a complete IELTS Academic Reading practice set.

TOPIC:
[INSERT TOPIC HERE]

========================
PASSAGE REQUIREMENTS
========================
- Length: 650–850 words
- Use formal academic tone
- Use natural and varied academic vocabulary
- Organize into clear paragraphs (3–5 paragraphs)
- Match the style and difficulty of Cambridge IELTS tests

========================
QUESTION REQUIREMENTS
========================
Create EXACTLY 13 questions.

- Include at least 5 different IELTS question types:
  • Matching Headings  
  • True / False / Not Given  
  • Multiple Choice  
  • Sentence Completion  
  • Summary Completion  
  • Short Answer Questions  
  • Matching Information / Features / Sentence Endings  
  • Table / Flow-chart / Note Completion  

- Questions must:
  • Be fair and not ambiguous  
  • Follow official IELTS format  
  • Avoid trick questions  
  • Be aligned with passage content  
  • Target difficulty: Band 6.5–7.5  

========================
OUTPUT RULES (VERY IMPORTANT)
========================
- Return ONLY valid JSON
- Do NOT include any explanation outside JSON
- Do NOT include markdown formatting (no **, no #, no ```)

========================
OUTPUT FORMAT
========================

{
  "title": "IELTS Academic Reading Practice",
  "topic": "[INSERT TOPIC HERE]",
  "passage": "Full reading passage here...",
  "questions": [
    {
      "number": 1,
      "type": "Matching Headings",
      "question": "Question text...",
      "options": ["A", "B", "C", "D"],
      "answer_format": "Write the correct letter..."
    }
  ],
  "answer_key": [
    {
      "number": 1,
      "answer": "B"
    }
  ],
  "explanations": [
    {
      "number": 1,
      "explanation": "Brief explanation for difficult questions..."
    }
  ]
}

parameters.content:
📘 IELTS Practice Bot (Telegram + AI)

This workflow automatically generates IELTS practice content:
- Monday → Grammar Practice
- Wednesday → Writing Task
- Friday → Reading Practice

The content is generated using AI and delivered via Telegram.

Flow:
Schedule Trigger → Select Test by Day → AI → Parse → Format → Split → Send Telegram


⚙️ Create Telegram BOT

1. Create a Telegram Bot using @BotFather
2. Copy your Bot Token
3. Add Telegram credentials in n8n
4. Add your AI (Gemini/OpenAI) credentials
5. Activate the workflow

Make sure your bot has started and received at least one message.

⚠️ Important:

You must replace the Chat ID in the Send nodes.

Example:
- Find: REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_CHAT_ID
- Replace with your own Chat ID

To get your Chat ID:
1. Send a message to your bot
2. Open:
https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/getUpdates
3. Copy "chat.id"

## Schedule Triggered

This workflow runs every day on a schedule. The output type changes depending on the day.

## AI Generated

This workflow uses AI to generate IELTS content.

Make sure to Connect your AI credentials (Gemini / OpenAI)

## Parse

Parse AI output (Gemini) from raw text into valid JSON.
Throws error if the response is not valid JSON

## Format Data into Telegram Message

Convert AI output into a readable Telegram message with formatting and question list

## Split Long Data

Split long Telegram message into smaller chunks to avoid message length limits.

## Send data to Telegram

Send the formatted grammar message to Telegram.

Don't forget to replace the Chat ID in the Send nodes.
